feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Alan Greenspan, former chairman of the Fed, dies at age 100
entry.content: Alan Greenspan presided over the Federal Reserve for 19 years under four presidents and mastered the art of obfuscation known as Fedspeak.

entry.title: Manchester United acquire land to build new 100,000 seater stadium
entry.content: Manchester United say they have secured the majority of the land required to build a new 100,000-seater stadium.The new stadium is planned to be the biggest sporting arena in the UK, with the club acq...

entry.title: Named George Washington? This World Cup promo could get you a Jeep
entry.content: Jeep is going to award 100 vehicles to eligible adults named George Washington, if Team USA wins the World Cup. But there are caveats.

entry.title: Former Wimbledon champion Vondrousova suspended 4 years for refusing doping test
entry.content: Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova was suspended for four years on Monday for refusing an anti-doping test — even though the Czech player cited “mental stress” and fear when the testing agent “rang my door late at night without properly identifying themselves.” The International Tennis Integrity Agency made the announcement, saying that Vondrousova refused a test in December, and that the decision was reached by an independent tribunal. Vondrousova, who won Wimbledon in 2023, reached a career-high ranking of No. 6 that year.

entry.title: Pirates Slide In Latest MLB Power Rankings, Playoff Chances Dicey
entry.content: The Pittsburgh Pirates (39-39) are in fourth place in the National League Central, 8.5 games behind the first-place Milwaukee Brewers.

entry.title: Brandon Aiyuk just wants the 49ers to mercifully fire him already
entry.content: Another week, another dicey social media post from the soon-to-be ex-Niner.

entry.title: 2026 World Cup today: Watch superstars Messi, Mbappé, Haaland play in matches on Boston 25
entry.content: Don’t miss a packed day of World Cup action, all leading into Boston Stadium's next big match on Tuesday.

entry.title: Braves vs Padres Prediction: Odds, recent stats, trends, and best bets for June 22
entry.content: Atlanta Braves vs San Diego Padres Game Preview

entry.title: The key differences between Tuchel and Southgate's England teams
entry.content: Analysing the similarities and differences between how Gareth Southgate and Thomas Tuchel led England.

entry.title: OpenAI signs deal to show Getty's images in ChatGPT results
entry.content: Getty Images will allow OpenAI to use its content library in AI search and ChatGPT.

entry.title: Chinese-style EV battery swap stations are coming to Europe
entry.content: Octopus and CATL are pledging to roll out a network of battery swap stations for heavy trucks across Europe.

entry.title: Morning Bid: Seventh in a decade
entry.content: Morning Bid: Seventh in a decade

entry.title: India’s Imports of Russian Oil Set for New Record High
entry.content: India’s Imports of Russian Oil Set for New Record High

entry.title: The Rule of 55 lets you tap your 401(k) penalty-free before 59½ — but less than a third of eligible workers use it
entry.content: The Rule of 55 lets you tap your 401(k) penalty-free before 59½ — but less than a third of eligible workers use it

entry.title: New York's congressional candidates make final case in last day before primary
entry.content: New York’s congressional candidates will deliver closing arguments ahead of primary elections Tuesday, as an ascendent progressive left takes on establishment Democrats

entry.title: Explainer-Three ways Trump could get a stake in AI firms for the US
entry.content: Explainer-Three ways Trump could get a stake in AI firms for the US

entry.title: Mortgage and refinance interest rates today, Monday, June 22, 2026: Purchase and refinance rate spread grows

entry.title: Best CD rates today, Monday, June 22, 2026: Lock in up to 4% APY
entry.content: Best CD rates today, Monday, June 22, 2026: Lock in up to 4% APY

entry.title: Judge in Charlie Kirk killing case to decide if prosecutors could be punished for comments in media
entry.content: The Utah judge in the murder case over Charlie Kirk’s killing says he will rule Monday whether prosecutors could face sanctions for comments to the media about a bullet fragment recovered from the conservative activist’s body

entry.title: Despite state bans, abortions have almost doubled. The reason? Pills via telehealth
entry.content: States that have banned abortion are suing to stop mailing of abortion pills over state lines. But the telehealth providers say no matter the outcome, they can adapt, and so will their patients.

entry.title: Despite state bans, abortions have almost doubled in the U.S.
entry.content: Four years ago, the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade. Yet there are nearly twice as many abortions in the U.S. as before. That's because abortion pills are now being prescribed online and mailed.

entry.title: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
entry.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced he would resign from office after facing a rebellion from members of his own party. Starmer became prime minister in 2024 after winning a landslide election.

entry.title: A blood test that screens for multiple types of cancer? It could be a reality soon
entry.content: The FDA is on the cusp of approving the first multi-cancer detection test, which could revolutionize the way health experts screen for cancers, ultimately dramatically improving survival rates.

entry.title: Serena Williams has returned to tennis. How returning to a sport can benefit you
entry.content: Serena Williams is back competing after years away from the tennis court. What lessons can the rest of us learn from that?

entry.title: Britain's economic woes fuel discontent with Brexit a decade after historic vote to leave EU
entry.content: Britain's economic woes fuel discontent with Brexit a decade after historic vote to leave EU

entry.title: Ethan Thornton is trying to do everything all at once
entry.content: Mach's approach differs sharply from some of its peers.

entry.title: Inside the Daring Operation to Avert a New Disaster at Chornobyl
entry.content: The battle to contain a fire at the site of the world’s worst nuclear disaster reveals a new danger on the front line of the war in Ukraine.

entry.title: China Slaps Restrictions on Dozens of U.S. Companies
entry.content: The tit-for-tat retaliation comes after Washington’s recent decision to expand its military-linked company list.

entry.title: As War With U.S. Eases, Iran Steps Up Hangings of Dissidents
entry.content: The regime is trying to consolidate its hold on power at home through fear.

entry.title: Colombia Elects Hardline Populist Who Vows War on Cocaine Gangs
entry.content: Abelardo de la Espriella promises more prisons and a crackdown after cocaine production and the ranks of armed groups expanded across the country.

entry.title: ‘Part of our culture’: Iran defying hardships and dreaming of first World Cup knockout round appearance
entry.content: Despite a war disrupting its soccer infrastructure, visa issues and travel restrictions, Iran will enter its final game of World Cup group play with a chance to move into the knockout round.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
We must always have old memories and young hopes.
Lucky Numbers: 43, 42, 74, 17, 51, 17

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Read the three and four-star reviews for the most reliable information on Amazon items.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: An average human loses about 200 head hairs per day.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Richard Bach
entry.content: “Argue for your limitations and sure enough, they're yours.” — Richard Bach

entry.title: It's not just about nudity warns actress - the complex reality of images and online abuse
entry.content: It's not just about nudity warns actress - the complex reality of images and online abuse

entry.title: 7 killed and dozens injured following series of weekend shootings in Chicago
entry.content: Police say a spate of shootings in Chicago has led to at least 38 injuries and seven deaths since Friday evening

entry.title: The public health team watching the World Cup
entry.content: As millions gather for the World Cup, a scrappy public health operation led by a team at Georgetown is tracking disease threats using surveys, wastewater and online chatter to spot outbreaks early.

entry.title: Trump Vents Growing Frustrations With Reflecting-Pool Problems
entry.content: The president said multiple people had been arrested for vandalism and the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool would likely need to be drained for repairs.

entry.title: Polymarket has reportedly been paying creators to post fake betting videos
entry.content: The Wall Street Journal reviewed 1,105 videos along with guidance given to creators for crafting their posts.

entry.title: 'Toy Story 5' goes to infinity and beyond at the box office
entry.content: Woody, Buzz Lightyear and Jessie are all back on the big screen in "Toy Story 5." The latest addition to the 30-year-old franchise earned $312 million at the global box office this weekend. The release marks 2026's biggest box office debut yet.

entry.title: Patriots HOF WR Gushes About Difficulty Of A.J. Brown, Mack Hollins Sharing Field
entry.content: Opposing defenses will have their hands full game-planning for A.J. Brown and Mack Hollins.

entry.title: NASCAR starting lineup for Cup Series race at San Diego in June 2026
entry.content: Check out the NASCAR Cup Series starting lineup for the Andruil 250 at San Diego on Sunday afternoon in June 2026.

entry.title: Kyle Stowers homers and Marlins beat Giants 2-1
entry.content: Kyle Stowers homered and scored on Otto López's go-ahead double to lead the Miami Marlins to a 2-1 win against the San Francisco Giants on Sunday. Ryan Gusto and four relievers limited the Giants to four hits as the Marlins won their eighth straight at home. Miami is a major league best 14-4 in June.

entry.title: Vinicius Oliveira calls for Josh Emmett or top 12 UFC featherweight
entry.content: With a successful UFC featherweight debut, Vinicius Oliveira targets a ranked opponent for his next bout.

entry.title: Contreras rips 3-run homer in 8-run inning as Gasser ends drought in Brewers' 9-4 win over Braves
entry.content: William Contreras had four hits, including a three-run homer in Milwaukee's eight-run second inning, and the Brewers beat the Atlanta Braves 9-4 on Sunday to snap a three-game skid. Sal Frelick added a two-run double off Bryce Elder (5-5) as the Brewers matched their season high for most runs in an inning.

entry.title: Phillies reportedly turn down Bryce Harper's outfield offer to help at trade deadline
entry.content: This is an interesting decision from the Phillies with Bryce Harper.

entry.title: Joshua Lane, Memphis Central lineman, commits to Tigers football, Charles Huff
entry.content: Memphis Central's class of 2027 defensive lineman Joshua Lane has committed to Memphis football. Lane does not have a 247Sports Composite ranking

entry.title: Winless Belgium being held by spirited Iran
entry.content: Listen to BBC Radio 5 Live commentary and follow live text updates as Belgium take on Iran in Group G at the World Cup.

entry.title: People ticketed for vandalizing Washington Reflecting Pool to be fully prosecuted, US Attorney Pirro says
entry.content: People ticketed for vandalizing Washington Reflecting Pool to be fully prosecuted, US Attorney Pirro says

entry.title: Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot dies in plane crash
entry.content: Claude Guillemot, who founded Ubisoft with his four brothers, has died at the age of 69.

entry.title: See the Global Chokepoints That Carry Much of the World’s Trade
entry.content: The Iran war highlights how vulnerable major trade corridors are to geopolitical tensions.

entry.title: Iran Says It Is Pausing Talks After Trump Threats
entry.content: The president warns the U.S. could strike Iran over its support for Hezbollah, the militant group Israel is fighting in Lebanon.

entry.title: Russia Faces Spreading Fuel Shortages After Ukrainian Drones Pummel Refineries
entry.content: Lines at gas stations are a growing headache for the Kremlin’s efforts to shield its citizens from the consequences of the four-year-old war.

entry.title: Polymarket reportedly paid creators to post deceptive videos about fake bets
entry.content: Many of those videos were reportedly filmed on “near-perfect copies” of the Polymarket website, while featuring trades and winnings that were not real.

entry.title: The Guardian view on nicotine: we shouldn’t buy the idea of addiction without harm | Editorial
entry.content: The UN is set to review the legal status of nicotine. An outright ban would go too far, but there is no case for its easy availabilityThe health case for banning cigarettes is ironclad. As the then head of the World Health Organization, Gro Harlem Brundtland, put it in 2000, “a cigarette is the only consumer product which when used as directed kills its consumer”. Smoking is still the leading cause of preventable death worldwide. Many countries, including the UK, have taken strong measures to restrict and even ban cigarettes and other tobacco products. Over the past two decades, however, tobacco-free nicotine products such as vapes and nicotine pouches, which use a synthetic version of the addictive ingredient, have exploded in popularity.Regulation has been slow. The nation of Palau has now tasked the WHO expert committee on drug dependence with reviewing nicotine, which will lead to a UN vote – likely to be in 2028 – on banning it worldwide. The case relies partly on deciding whether addiction and dependence themselves – in the absence of other major health consequences – are harmful. There is certainly an argument for that, and smoking taught us that it is often better to stamp out highly addictive habits if consequences may become obvious later. But there is also reason for caution. Continue reading...

entry.title: TechCrunch Mobility: A new robotaxi scorecard shows China’s dominance
entry.content: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

entry.title: How much preventive health screening should I be getting?
entry.content: Screenings can find treatable conditions before they’ve caused too much damage – but ‘overscreening’ can cause harmI couldn’t help but roll my eyes when tech entrepreneur and longevity influencer Bryan Johnson posted about his girlfriend’s “vaginal microbiome report” in April. (He said it was in the “top 1% of vaginas”.) While the vaginal microbiome is genuinely interesting, most clinicians don’t routinely recommend this test to patients.As medical technology has become more powerful – and more marketable – the line between helpful screening and unnecessary testing has blurred. Continue reading...

entry.content: Plus, the lucrative Polymarket bets that never were, and what to know about shark encounters.

entry.title: NASA's Perseverance rover has traveled the distance of a marathon on Mars
entry.content: It did so in just five years.

entry.title: When the Trump administration cracks down on Anthropic, who benefits?
entry.content: On the new episode of Equity, we discussed what actually prompted the administration's latest moves against Anthropic, and what this might mean for the AI ecosystem.

entry.title: Trump threatens Iran over Hezbollah-Israel conflict as Vance meets delegations in Switzerland
entry.content: Vice President JD Vance meets with officials from Pakistan, Qatar and Iran for talks in Lucerne, Switzerland as Iran threatened to close down the Strait of Hormuz after Israel’s strikes on Lebanon. NBC News’ Keir Simmons and Gary Grumbach report on President Donald Trump’s intentions to reset relations with Tehran and his threats to attack again if the Hezbollah and Israel conflict escalates.

entry.title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
entry.content: Siri’s AI overhaul may have grabbed the headlines at WWDC, but some of Apple’s most useful AI features are arriving elsewhere in iOS 27.

entry.title: 2026 NHL draft: Blackhawks could still be beneficiaries at fourth overall
entry.content: The Chicago Blackhawks were a bit unlucky with the NHL draft lottery, but they still have an excellent opportunity to select a game-changer at fourth overall in the 2026 NHL draft. Today, we look at the five best options for the Blackhawks.

entry.title: The Latest: Vance meets Iranian negotiators in Switzerland to work on details of deal
entry.content: U.S. and Iranian negotiators are in Switzerland for talks to work on key details of their interim agreement to end the Iran war

entry.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
entry.content: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades raceIn the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza, first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire.Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

entry.title: Cote d'Ivoire coach Emerse Faé hits out at Germany's "lack of fair play" after defeat
entry.content: Côte d'Ivoire coach Emerse Faé believed Germany showed a "lack of fair play" during Saturday's World Cup clash in Toronto, which saw his team lose 1-2.After Côte d'Ivoire defender Wilfried Singo pu...

entry.title: 'Every moment ached with importance as Northampton win compelling final'
entry.content: Northampton show their grit and unity to prevail in a tussle of a Prem final with Exeter and secure their third English title.

entry.content: Manchester United are closing in on their second signing of the summer.Last week, Tottenham Hotspur entered the race to sign Mateus Fernandes ahead of United, with a report suggesting they are nearing...

entry.title: In Taylor Swift's beach town, every clue becomes a wedding rumor
entry.content: Rumors that Taylor Swift was planning to marry at her Rhode Island home this weekend have drawn fans, photographers and curious visitors to the seaside village of Watch Hill after a large tent appeared near her estate

entry.title: Canada’s policies force asylum seekers into US to face deportation, critics say
entry.content: Advocates say the Safe Third Country Agreement forces immigrants to head to an unsafe country: the United StatesIt was the threat of gang violence in Honduras that pushed Carlos and Antonia to flee their home. In 2021, with their toddler, Alejandro, and a handful of belongings, the married couple ventured north hoping to reach safety in the US.The journey, through Guatemala and Mexico, was filled with danger and uncertainty Continue reading...

entry.title: ‘A genuine wildlife emergency’: everything you need to know about the arrival of H5 bird flu in Australia
entry.content: What is the virus? Where did it come from? Are humans at risk? What does it mean for the agricultural sector and wildlife? Experts answer your questionsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastA deadly strain of bird flu, known as H5 bird flu, has arrived on the Australian mainland.The federal agriculture minister, Julie Collins, this weekend confirmed that a brown skua – that had been found sick in Western Australia – had died from the H5N1 virus. The skua is a wild migratory bird. Continue reading...

entry.title: How location sharing helped police catch a serial rapist-turned-killer
entry.content: The mother of murdered model Christy Giles pleads for others to share their locations. She says the technology helped police catch David Pearce, who murdered Giles and her friend, architect Hilda Marcela Cabrales.

entry.title: "Dead Girls Don't Talk"
entry.content: A man charged with murder for poisoning two women tells a friend, "dead girls don't talk." Brave survivors speak out on their behalf. "48 Hours" contributor Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

entry.title: FAA investigates incident that forced Delta flight to abort landing
entry.content: The Federal Aviation Administration is investigating a close call between two commercial flights that forced one to abort landing in order to avoid collision

entry.title: Colombia’s runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
entry.content: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups Colombians go to the polls on Sunday in a presidential runoff expected to trigger to a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc).Polls show the frontrunner is the Trump-admiring far-right lawyer and millionaire businessman Abelardo de la Espriella, who has vowed to abandon President Gustavo Petro’s “total peace” plan of negotiating the disarmament of all criminal organisations and instead return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups. Continue reading...

entry.title: Newsom declares state of emergency in Los Angeles for Boyle Heights warehouse fire
entry.content: Firefighters faced renewed challenges Saturday at a large Boyle Heights warehouse fire, where conditions remain highly complex as Mayor Karen Bass declared a local emergency to support response efforts.

entry.title: Timeline: Investigating the Long Island serial killings
entry.content: What began as a search for one missing woman — Shannan Gilbert — led to multiple bodies and the capture of Rex Heuermann.

entry.title: US Vice President JD Vance arrives in Switzerland to launch talks with Iran on its nuclear program
entry.content: U.S. Vice President JD Vance has landed in Switzerland to help launch negotiations with Iranian leaders over Tehran’s nuclear program

entry.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
entry.content: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country.For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conservative government. Continue reading...
